title: High-Efficiency 48V → 12V/20A
description: A high-efficiency synchronous forward converter delivering 240 W at 12 V/20 A from a 48 V bus, targeting data-centre and telecom intermediate bus applications where every percent of efficiency matters.
design_notes: The forward topology is preferred over the flyback at 240 W because energy is transferred directly to the secondary during the switch-on phase, reducing RMS currents in the transformer windings and allowing a smaller core for the same power. Synchronous rectification on the secondary eliminates the ~0.5 V Schottky forward drop: at 20 A, this saves 10 W — an improvement from roughly 88 % to 90 % efficiency. The 2 % leakage ratio assumes a tightly wound interleaved transformer (bifilar or sectioned winding); the RCD clamp power dissipation is minimal. The 200 kHz switching frequency is a deliberate middle ground: low enough that the synchronous rectifier's gate-drive losses stay below 1 W, yet high enough that the inductor (approx 10–15 µH) fits in a compact package. Duty-cycle range is approximately 0.23–0.27 across the input span — very narrow, which simplifies compensator design.
source: Erickson & Maksimovic §6.3; TI SLUA107 — Forward Converter Design; Vicor AN008 — 48V IBA
